mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing the 4.0% threshold signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly declines in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures indicate a broad demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 55% or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with directional accuracy below 52% or t-statistic < 1.5 on forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 0.3% monthly threshold raises expectations for central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3% monthly are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor demand and higher household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling housing starts and rising personal consumption expenditures signals a shift from interest-rate-sensitive housing demand to resilient consumer spending, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence pattern fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with directional accuracy below 50% or the interaction term loses significance with p > 0.05 in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production outpacing real GDP growth by more than one percentage point signals supply-side bottlenecks and inventory overhangs that suppress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-GDPC1 spread fails to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 53% or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.
